name: Arugula

supplementary_research.finding: Erucin, the dominant isothiocyanate in arugula (Eruca sativa), inhibits proliferation of MCF7 breast cancer cells at 28 µM (IC50) via G2/M cell-cycle arrest, suppression of microtubule dynamics, and a 5–9-fold increase in apoptosis at 25 µM. Authors suggest dietary arugula consumption may help prevent or retard growth of pre-malignant cells.

Glucosinolates from cruciferous vegetables (including arugula's glucoerucin and gluconapin) are hydrolysed to isothiocyanates that activate the Nrf2/ARE pathway, inducing phase-2 detoxification enzymes. Higher cruciferous intake correlates with reduced risk of colorectal, lung, and prostate cancers in epidemiological cohorts.

Arugula juice contains among the highest dietary nitrate concentrations of any leafy green (~250 mg NO3⁻/100 g fresh weight). Dietary nitrate is reduced to nitric oxide in vivo, improving endothelial function, lowering blood pressure, and enhancing mitochondrial efficiency — all mechanisms relevant to cardiovascular aging.

culinary_pairings.source: Insalata di Rucola — classic Italian salad; fat in olive oil enhances fat-soluble vitamin K and carotenoid absorption
Acid brightens bitter glucosinolates; walnuts add ALA omega-3 for synergistic anti-inflammatory effect
Classic antipasto combination; sweetness of fig balances arugula's bite; pairs well on pizza or bruschetta

seasonality: Cool-season annual; peak spring and autumn; bolt-prone in summer heat

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Converted to nitric oxide via entero-salivary cycle; bioavailability enhanced by chewing and oral microbiome; reduced by mouthwash use
Hydrolysed by myrosinase on cell disruption (chewing, chopping); cooking inactivates myrosinase but gut microbiome retains partial conversion capacity
Fat-soluble; absorption markedly improved when eaten with olive oil or other dietary fat; supports bone mineralisation and vascular calcification prevention
Supports one-carbon metabolism; important for DNA methylation fidelity, homocysteine clearance, and neural function; heat-sensitive, best consumed raw

synergies.description: Dietary fat (oleic acid in EVOO) dramatically increases absorption of arugula's fat-soluble vitamins K and carotenoids (lutein, beta-carotene), and may enhance bioavailability of glucosinolate breakdown products

Broccoli myrosinase (active enzyme) can hydrolyse glucosinolates in cooked arugula, rescuing isothiocyanate formation when arugula is lightly wilted; combining raw broccoli sprouts with arugula maximises erucin and sulforaphane yield
Both are nitrate-rich; combining arugula with beetroot provides additive nitric oxide substrate, potentially amplifying blood-pressure-lowering and endothelial benefits beyond either alone
